_comment: Agnostic template used for any merchant that has NOT yet been researched. Bracketed placeholders [merchant], [product], [MAU], etc. are visible on purpose so it is obvious what still needs per-merchant research to fill. Once the Supabase merchant table is populated, these fields are overridden per slug.
PAIN_1_DESC: Heavy reliance on a single processor for [merchant]'s key payment flows — any outage or policy change instantly suspends recurring revenue.
PAIN_2_DESC: [MAU count]+ active users across [# countries]+ countries, yet checkout still routes through centralized, US-first acquiring — costly and failure-prone cross-border.
PAIN_3_DESC: Thin local payment coverage outside cards — missing UPI in India, Pix in Brazil, wallets across APAC — directly capping [merchant]'s global conversion.
PAIN_4_DESC: No smart routing or cascade retries across acquirers, so declined transactions fall straight to lost revenue instead of being recovered.
PAIN_5_DESC: Interchange, cross-border and FX fees are negotiated per processor with no competitive leverage, leaving material margin on the table at [merchant]'s volume.
CAPABILITY_1_DESC: Per-transaction decisioning across every acquirer, lifting [merchant]'s auth rate on [key product flow] without a single engineering sprint.
CAPABILITY_2_DESC: Automatic cascade across processors rescues declined transactions in real time, turning involuntary churn into recovered revenue.
CAPABILITY_3_DESC: 1,000+ payment methods, wallets and local rails — UPI, Pix, iDEAL, Konbini, GrabPay — live through one integration, unlocking [merchant]'s global conversion.
CAPABILITY_4_DESC: One reconciliation, one analytics layer, one contract surface across every PSP and market, replacing a fragmented ops mesh with a single control plane.
